questions.question: Explain the concept of encapsulation in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Encapsulation is bundling data and methods into a single unit (class)
It restricts direct access to some components
Example: class with private data and public methods to access it

questions.question: What is the difference between public, private, and protected access specifiers?
keyPoints: Public: accessible from anywhere
Private: accessible only within the class
Protected: accessible within class and derived classes

questions.question: Explain the purpose of 'this' pointer in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: 'this' is an implicit pointer pointing to current object
It is used to distinguish between member variables and parameters with same name
Example: this->x = x;

questions.question: What are constructors and destructors? Explain their types.
keyPoints: Constructor: special member function for object initialization, called automatically
Types: Default, Parameterized, Copy constructors
Destructor: called automatically when object goes out of scope, performs cleanup
Only one destructor per class, no return type

questions.question: Define a class in C++ and explain its components with syntax.
keyPoints: Class is user-defined data type using 'class' keyword
Components: data members, member functions, access specifiers
Syntax: class ClassName { private: ... public: ... };

questions.question: Explain the difference between class and object with an example.
keyPoints: Class: blueprint/template defining structure
Object: instance of class with actual memory
Example: class Car defines blueprint; Car BMW creates object

questions.question: Explain the different types of constructors in C++ with examples. Also explain when destructors are called.
keyPoints: Default constructor: no parameters, initializes members to default values
Parameterized constructor: accepts arguments for initialization
Copy constructor: initializes object using another object of same class
Destructor: called when object goes out of scope, deleted, or program ends

questions.question: Write a C++ program to create a class called 'Student' with appropriate data members and member functions to accept and display student details.
keyPoints: Define class Student with private: name, rollno, marks; public: accept(), display() functions
Use constructors for initialization
Demonstrate object creation and function calls
Show proper use of access specifiers

questions.question: Explain the following: (a) Static data members (b) Friend functions (c) Inline member functions
keyPoints: Static: shared across all objects, single copy in memory
Friend: non-member function with access to private members
Inline: functions defined inside class definition, compiler may expand at call site

questions.question: Explain the OOP principles and how C++ implements them. Discuss how classes help in implementing these principles.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: bundling data and methods in class
Inheritance: deriving new classes from existing ones
Polymorphism: same interface different behavior through function overloading/virtual functions
Classes serve as foundation for all OOP concepts in C++
